answer.text: The change in delta for a unit change in the price of the underlying asset
The change in premium per day of time elapsed
The change in premium per unit change in volatility
The change in premium per change in interest rate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Gamma = Change in delta / Unit change in underlying price. It is the second derivative — measuring how fast delta itself changes as the underlying moves. **The Trap:** Time elapsed is Theta. Volatility change is Vega. Interest rate change is Rho. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII and IV cover gamma in the context of option portfolio risk management. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Gamma is highest for ATM options near expiry — this is why ATM options near expiry are the most sensitive to price movements and require the most active hedging.
